previewTitle.value: Spike Lee & Kyle Bell - Rolex mentoring programme - Video

description.value: Rolex Mentor and Protégé in Film, 2020–2022

text.value: Spike Lee, one of today’s most socially conscious filmmakers, chose to mentor Kyle Bell, a young filmmaker from the Thlopthlocco Creek Tribal Town of Oklahoma, to help expand the boundaries of his evocative filmmaking. Lee notes how the experience of Native Americans is similar to that of Africans transported as slaves to the US, and Bell’s story and film work moved Lee to choose him as his protégé in the 2020−2022 cycle of the Rolex mentoring programme. During the mentoring period, Bell watched Lee make a documentary and spent time with him in the editing room. For the protégé, the greatest lesson was the mentor encouraging him to find and follow his voice in telling the stories of his people. “He has helped me be vocal about my vision and lead other people along the way,” says Bell, whose ultimate goal is to expand from documentaries and enter into the more narrative world that Lee is part of.

pageTitle.value: An interview with Martin Scorsese - Rolex and Cinema

pageCaption.value: To future filmmakers Martin Scorsese says: “Don’t be afraid to try anything, no matter how crazy it might seem to others.” These are encouraging words from this director, producer and screenwriter who is responsible for many of the biggest classics in film history. He is always pushing boundaries and helping to promote the creative spark in others.

WaPageTitle.value: An interview with Martin Scorsese - Rolex and Cinema

metadescription.value: Martin Scorsese's career has spanned 50 years and as many films. Watch an interview with the director, producer and screenwriter now.

previewTitle.value: An interview with Martin Scorsese - Rolex and Cinema

pageTitle.value: An interview with James Cameron - Rolex and Cinema

pageCaption.value: James Cameron is acknowledged for using trail-blazing technology in his films, but when it comes to advice for emerging filmmakers he says to “stay in touch with the human heart”. The director of Titanic and Avatar believes nothing is more important than “speaking with an authentic voice and portraying the human condition at its most elemental level”.

WaPageTitle.value: An interview with James Cameron - Rolex and Cinema

metadescription.value: Acclaimed director James Cameron is responsible for some of the most memorable films of the past three decades. Watch the interview with him now.

pageTitle.value: Phyllida Lloyd & Whitney White - Rolex mentoring programme - Video

pageCaption.value: Drawn together by a mutual love of Shakespeare and of music, as well as an interest in telling women’s stories, Phyllida Lloyd, London-based director of such worldwide hits as the musical and film ‘Mamma Mia’, and American director, actor and musician Whitney White, found that their relationship in the 2020−2022 cycle of the Rolex Mentor and Protégé Arts Initiative quickly grew.

WaPageTitle.value: Phyllida Lloyd and Whitney White - Video

metadescription.value: Phyllida Lloyd and protégée Whitney White's mentorship grew as they explored the influences shaping today’s stage. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Carrie Mae Weems & Camila Rogríguez Triana - Rolex mentoring programme - Video

pageCaption.value: Carrie Mae Weems was drawn to choose Camila Rodríguez Triana as a protégée in the 2020−2022 cycle of the Rolex Mentor and Protégé Arts Initiative, as, although the young Colombian visual artist came from a different country and different world, there was a deep connection.

pageTitle.value: Lin-Manuel Miranda & Agustina San Martín - Rolex mentoring programme - Video

pageCaption.value: As part of the 2020−2022 cycle of the Rolex mentoring programme, Lin-Manuel Miranda and his protégée Argentinian filmmaker Agustina San Martín met while he was making his film directorial debut after a string of Tony award-winning musicals including ‘Hamilton’ and ‘In the Heights’.
